title: excoecaria marginata var. obovata
intro: Excoecaria marginata var. obovata, also known as the 'giant lacquer tree', is a plant species native to tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands, characterized by its dark green, elliptical leaves and milky sap. The milky sap of this plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Excoecaria marginata var. obovata include reducing inflammation and pain, treating wounds and infections, and even exhibiting anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
